section_name: Alias Driver’s License for Law Enforcement Purposes
text:
(a) After written approval by the director, the department may issue to a law enforcement officer an alias driver’s license to be used in supervised activities involving a criminal investigation.

(b) An application for, or possession or use of, an alias driver’s license for a purpose described by this section by the officer to whom the license is issued is not a violation of this subchapter unless the department has canceled, suspended, or revoked the license.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.452 — Alias Driver’s License for Law Enforcement Purposes

(a) After written approval by the director, the department may issue to a law enforcement officer an alias driver’s license to be used in supervised activities involving a criminal investigation.

(b) An application for, or possession or use of, an alias driver’s license for a purpose described by this section by the officer to whom the license is issued is not a violation of this subchapter unless the department has canceled, suspended, or revoked the license.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: After written approval by the director, the department may issue to a law enforcement officer an alias driver’s license to be used in supervised activities involving a criminal investigation.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: An application for, or possession or use of, an alias driver’s license for a purpose described by this section by the officer to whom the license is issued is not a violation of this subchapter unless the department has canceled, suspended, or revoked the license.
